description: A fragrant ointment for healing or soothing; what comforts through touch. 'Moli' names what soothes—the gentle application that eases pain.
sound_symbolism: The word soothes gentle. 'Mo' grounds with calm stability, comfort present. Then 'li' flows with gentle application, pain easing.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo moli' (the balm). Applied externally unlike 'nepha' (medicine). For wounds, soreness, skin ailments.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Balms soothed wounds—herbal preparations applied with care, healing through touch.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Words as balm—gentle speech that soothes emotional wounds, comfort through communication.
